title: teucrium polium subvar. homotrichum
intro: Teucrium polium subvar. homotrichum, also known as the Greek germander or wild marjoram, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Teucrium polium, native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Teucrium polium subvar. homotrichum include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Teucrium polium subvar. homotrichum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, and can also be used in traditional herbal medicine and cooking.
